long_description:
Preprocessing step that centers and/or normalizes data to improve model performance. Essential preparation for multivariate calibration methods.

Centering:
- Subtracts column means from all samples
- Removes baseline/offset effects
- Centers data around zero
- Recommended for most applications

Normalization (Autoscaling):
- Divides by column standard deviations
- Scales each variable to unit variance
- Gives equal weight to all variables
- Important when variables have different scales/units
- Particularly useful for spectroscopic data

Combination Effects:

Centering Only:
- Removes offset differences between samples
- Preserves variance structure
- Good when variables are on same scale

Normalization Only (rare):
- Without centering, less common
- Useful for specific data distributions

Both Centering + Normalization (recommended):
- Standard preprocessing for chemometrics
- Equal treatment of all variables
- Improves model stability and interpretability
- Required for most PLS and PCR applications

Multiway Data:
- Works with 2D, 3D, and higher dimensional data
- Direction parameter specifies axis
- Default direction (-1) applies along last axis

Note: Validation data will be transformed using calibration statistics during modeling.

layout.name: direction
layout.label: Direction (for multiway, -1=default)
layout.tooltip: Dimension along which to center/normalize (-1 for default)

layout.name: nway_flag
layout.label: Data Dimensionality
layout.tooltip: Automatically inherited from Load Data function
